steps.description: Demultiplexing and alignment with Cell Ranger
steps.code_example:
# Process 10x Genomics scRNA-seq with Cell Ranger
cellranger count \
  --id=sample_id \
  --transcriptome=/path/to/refdata-gex-GRCh38 \
  --fastqs=/path/to/fastqs/ \
  --sample=sample_name \
  --localcores=16 \
  --localmem=64

steps.description: Quality control and filtering with Scanpy
steps.code_example:
import scanpy as sc

def run_scanpy_pipeline(h5ad_path):
    """Standard scRNA-seq analysis with Scanpy."""
    adata = sc.read_h5ad(h5ad_path)

    # QC and filtering
    sc.pp.filter_cells(adata, min_genes=200)
    sc.pp.filter_genes(adata, min_cells=3)
    adata.var['mt'] = adata.var_names.str.startswith('MT-')
    sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata, qc_vars=['mt'], inplace=True)
    adata = adata[adata.obs.pct_counts_mt < 20].copy()

    # Normalize and log-transform
    sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1e4)
    sc.pp.log1p(adata)

    # Feature selection, PCA, neighbors, clustering
    sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata, n_top_genes=2000)
    sc.tl.pca(adata, n_comps=50)
    sc.pp.neighbors(adata, n_pcs=30)
    sc.tl.leiden(adata, resolution=0.5)
    sc.tl.umap(adata)

    sc.pl.umap(adata, color=['leiden'], save='_clusters.png')
    return adata

steps.description: Dimensionality reduction and clustering with scVI
steps.code_example:
import scvi
import scanpy as sc

def run_scvi_integration(adata, batch_key='batch'):
    """Batch integration with scVI."""
    scvi.model.SCVI.setup_anndata(adata, batch_key=batch_key)
    model = scvi.model.SCVI(adata, n_latent=30, n_layers=2)
    model.train(max_epochs=200, early_stopping=True)

    # Get latent representation
    adata.obsm['X_scVI'] = model.get_latent_representation()

    # Use scVI latent space for clustering
    sc.pp.neighbors(adata, use_rep='X_scVI')
    sc.tl.leiden(adata)
    sc.tl.umap(adata)
    return adata, model
